items.body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## üéØ Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue requests documentation updates for `Azure.Core` ‚Äî specifically adding a CHANGELOG entry for credential schema definitions being merged into `ConfigurationSchema.json`, and updating the README to reflect configuration schema support, DI integration, and the Azure.Identity credential types now provided by Azure.Core.

<details>
<summary>üìã Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Core` (latest: 1.52.0/1.53.0-beta.1)
**Affected API:** `ConfigurationSchema.json`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`
**Scenarios:**
- CHANGELOG 1.53.0-beta.1 `Features Added` section is empty; credential schema merge not listed as a user-visible feature
- README has no mention of `ConfigurationSchema.json` NuGet-embedded schema for `appsettings.json` IntelliSense
- README Key concepts section does not reflect that Azure.Identity credential types now live in Azure.Core

**Root ask:** Update CHANGELOG to move the credential schema merge entry to `Features Added`, and update README to document configuration schema support, DI integration, and credential type changes.
</details>

<details>
<summary>üîé Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only change triggered by commit `30f0d27fb` (PR #58009), which merged Azure.Identity credential configuration schema definitions into `Azure.Core`'s `ConfigurationSchema.json`.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md` ‚Äî verify the 1.53.0-beta.1 `Features Added` section and move the credential schema merge entry from `Other Changes` to `Features Added`
2. Review `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` ‚Äî add a section describing `ConfigurationSchema.json` support for `appsettings.json` IntelliSense and reference `ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md`
3. Update the README Key concepts section to mention Azure.Identity credential types (`DefaultAzureCredential`, etc.) that now originate from Azure.Core
</details>

<details>
<summary>üè∑Ô∏è Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` ‚Äî This is an Azure SDK client library documentation issue; not management plane, not provisioning
- **Service:** `Azure.Core` ‚Äî Explicitly named in the issue title and body as the affected package
- **Confidence:** High ‚Äî The issue directly and unambiguously names `Azure.Core` in `sdk/core/Azure.Core/`, with a clear client library categorization. No ambiguity between services or categories.
</details>

<details>
<summary>üë• Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Azure.Core` (line 60) ‚Äî matched because the predicted service label is "Azure.Core"
- **AzureSdkOwners:** `@m-nash`
- **ServiceOwners:** none listed
- **Routing action:** Assigned `@m-nash` (single AzureSdkOwner). "needs-team-attention" label not added because this is a bot-filed issue, not customer-reported.
- **Scan notes:** Scan started from end of CODEOWNERS (line 1228) upward. Passed through `%CodeGen %Mgmt` (line 1221), `%CodeGen` (line 1218), all management-specific entries, the `%Mgmt` catch-all (line 903), all other service labels ‚Äî none matched. Matched `%Azure.Core` (line 60) as first match. Stopped there.
</details>
